verdicts.reasoning: The preg_replace at init.php:724 uses a literal pattern '/\<br\s*\/\>/i' with NO /e modifier, so it cannot execute code. The tainted value ($sitename from get_option('blogname')) flows into an email body sent via wp_mail(), not into any command execution sink. preg_replace without /e is a string replacement operation, not RCE. Additionally, blogname is only settable by admins.

Same as loginizer-rce-001. The preg_replace at init.php:725 uses pattern '/(?<!<br\/>)\n/i' with NO /e modifier. This is a simple newline-to-br conversion for HTML email formatting. No code execution possible. The scanner incorrectly flagged preg_replace as an RCE sink.

HybridAuth Logger (lib/hybridauth/Logger/Logger.php:127) writes $_SERVER['REMOTE_ADDR'] to a log file via file_put_contents(). The file path ($this->file) is set during Logger construction from HybridAuth config, not user input. REMOTE_ADDR is set by the web server, not directly controllable by the client. The log file is a plain text file (not .php), so even if an attacker could inject content, it would not be executed. This is standard logging behavior in a third-party library.

The sink at main/settings/2fa.php:406 outputs either 'selected' or '' based on a strict comparison ($enforce_type === 'strict_enforce'). The tainted value is never directly echoed - only the result of a boolean comparison is output. Additionally, $enforce_type passes through sanitize_text_field() at line 284. The page requires manage_options capability (line 12). Scanner incorrectly marked auth_required as false.

Same pattern as xss-001. The sink at main/settings/2fa.php:409 outputs 'selected' or '' based on strict comparison ($enforce_type === 'grace_enforce'). The tainted value is never echoed directly. Admin-only page.
Same pattern. Sink at 2fa.php:415 outputs 'style="display:none;"' or '' based on comparison ($enforce_type !== 'grace_enforce'). Hardcoded output strings, no tainted data in output. Admin-only.

Sink at 2fa.php:418 uses esc_attr() on the output value. The scanner's own finding shows esc_attr() is applied: echo !empty($enforce_reg['2fa_enforce']['time']) ? esc_attr($enforce_reg['2fa_enforce']['time']) : '1'. This IS properly sanitized. Admin-only page.
Same as xss-003. Outputs hardcoded 'style="display:none;"' or '' based on comparison. No user data in output. Admin-only.

Sink at 2fa.php:486 uses lz_htmlizer() which calls htmlentities($string, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'). This IS an effective XSS sanitizer for HTML context output. The $_POST value branch also goes through lz_htmlizer(). Admin-only page (manage_options check at line 12). Scanner incorrectly marked auth_required as false.
Same as xss-006. Uses lz_htmlizer() (htmlentities with ENT_QUOTES) for output sanitization. Admin-only page.

Dashboard page (dashboard.php) requires manage_options (line 12). The $_SERVER['SERVER_ADDR'] at line 262 is set by the web server, not user-controllable. REMOTE_ADDR, HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR, HTTP_CLIENT_IP all use esc_html() (lines 270-272). lz_optpost('lz_custom_ip_method') calls lz_htmlizer() which does htmlentities(). The lz_getip() return value is validated by lz_valid_ip() which rejects non-IP strings. Scanner incorrectly marked auth_required as false.

Sink at social_login.php:28 uses isset($_GET['settings']) which only checks for existence, then outputs hardcoded strings 'nav-tab-active' or ''. The value of $_GET['settings'] is never echoed. Admin-only page (manage_options at line 9).

fopen($_FILES['lz_import_file_csv']['tmp_name']) at brute-force.php:353 is standard PHP file upload handling. The tmp_name is set by PHP itself (points to the temporary upload directory), not user-controllable for path traversal. The file is only opened for reading ('r' mode). Admin-only page with nonce verification (check_admin_referer). Extension validated to be 'csv' at line 351.
Sink at brute-force.php:511 outputs $loginizer['reset_retries']/60/60 which is an arithmetic division operation on a numeric option value. The result is always a number. Admin-only page.

Sink at brute-force.php:675 uses lz_optpost('max_retries', $loginizer['max_retries']). lz_optpost() applies lz_htmlizer() (htmlentities) and lz_inputsec() (addslashes). The default value $loginizer['max_retries'] is a numeric option. Admin-only page.
Sink at brute-force.php:681 outputs arithmetic: ($loginizer['lockout_time']) / 60 - always numeric result. Admin-only page.
Same as xss-011. lz_optpost() sanitizes with htmlentities. Numeric default value. Admin-only.
Arithmetic division on numeric value. Admin-only page.
Arithmetic division on numeric value. Admin-only page.

Sink at brute-force.php:706 echoes $loginizer['notify_email'] which is an email address stored by admin. Even if not escaped, this is an admin-only page and the value is admin-controlled. Self-XSS at worst, which is not a vulnerability.
Same as xss-016. Admin-stored email address displayed on admin-only page.
lz_POSTchecked() outputs either 'checked="checked"' or '' - hardcoded strings. No user data in output. Admin-only.
Same as xss-018. lz_POSTchecked() outputs hardcoded strings only. Admin-only.
Sink at brute-force.php:1066 explicitly uses esc_attr(): echo (empty($saved_msgs['inv_userpass']) ? '' : esc_attr($saved_msgs['inv_userpass'])). Properly sanitized. Admin-only.
Sink at brute-force.php:1067 echoes $loginizer['d_msg']['inv_userpass'] which is a hardcoded default message string set by the plugin itself (not user input). Admin-only page.
Uses esc_attr() for output. Admin-only page. Same as xss-020.
Hardcoded default message string (d_msg), not user input. Admin-only page.
Uses esc_attr() for output. Admin-only page.
Hardcoded default message string (d_msg). Admin-only page.
Uses esc_attr() for output. Admin-only page.
Uses esc_attr() for output. Admin-only page.
Uses esc_attr() for output. Admin-only page.
lz_POSTchecked() outputs hardcoded 'checked="checked"' or '' only. Admin-only page.
lz_POSTchecked() outputs hardcoded strings only. Admin-only page.
lz_POSTchecked() outputs hardcoded strings only. Admin-only page.
Uses esc_html() on both $_POST and stored values. Admin-only page.
Uses esc_html() on both $_POST and stored values. Admin-only page.

Sink at passwordless.php:128 uses lz_htmlizer() which calls htmlentities(ENT_QUOTES, UTF-8) - effective XSS sanitization. Page requires manage_options (line 12). Scanner incorrectly marked auth_required as false.
Same as xss-034. lz_htmlizer() provides htmlentities sanitization. Admin-only page.
Same as xss-034. lz_htmlizer() provides htmlentities sanitization. Admin-only page.
Sink at security.php:571 uses lz_htmlizer() which provides htmlentities sanitization. Page requires manage_options (line 12 of security.php). Scanner incorrectly marked auth_required as false.

Sink at security.php:904 outputs 'checked' or false based on !empty() comparison. The tainted value is never directly echoed - only a boolean result mapped to a hardcoded string. Admin-only page. Scanner incorrectly marked auth_required as false.
Same as xss-038. Outputs 'checked' or false based on strict string comparison to 'block'. No user data in output. Admin-only.
Same as xss-039. Outputs 'checked' or false based on comparison to 'destroy'. Admin-only.
Sink at security.php:924 explicitly uses esc_attr() on both $_POST and stored values. Properly sanitized. Admin-only page. Scanner incorrectly marked auth_required as false.

summary: All 45 scanner findings for Loginizer are false positives. The two 'critical' RCE findings flagged preg_replace as a code execution sink, but none use the /e modifier - they are simple string replacements in email formatting. All XSS findings target admin-only settings pages protected by current_user_can('manage_options'), and most sinks use proper sanitization (esc_attr, esc_html, lz_htmlizer/htmlentities, or output only hardcoded strings from boolean comparisons). The scanner systematically misidentified auth requirements, marking admin-only pages as unauthenticated. No wp_ajax_nopriv_ handlers or REST API routes exist in this plugin.
feedback: Scanner has major issues: (1) Flagging preg_replace without /e modifier as RCE - this is a fundamental misunderstanding of PHP security. (2) Systematically marking admin settings pages as auth_required:false despite clear current_user_can('manage_options') checks at the top of every settings function. (3) Flagging outputs that use esc_attr(), esc_html(), or htmlentities() as missing sanitization. (4) Treating get_option() values displayed on admin-only pages as exploitable XSS - even if unsanitized, admin self-XSS via stored options is not a real vulnerability. (5) Flagging boolean comparison outputs (=== 'value' ? 'selected' : '') as XSS when no user data reaches the output.
